summary: Removed several NON_MATCHING assembly fallbacks by getting the C implementations to match in DSP init, PPC EABI C++ constructor init, ground stage position setup, and HSD shadow allocation. The PR deleted large inline-asm blocks and some companion asm data/sbss constants, replacing them with C-side declarations, pragmas, helper factoring, and literal/float-constant workarounds.
what_changed: Changed `__DSP_init_flag` in `src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.c` from `extern BOOL __DSP_init_flag;` to a definition `BOOL __DSP_init_flag;`, removing the need for the sbss definition in `asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp.s`.

Made `DSPInit` use C instead of its asm fallback. The old comment said it was non-matching due to externs; the fix defines the init flag in C and calls `__DSP_debug_printf` with explicit string literals for the build date/time instead of referencing `lbl_80400C08`, `__DATE__`, and `__TIME__`.
Deleted DSP asm-side data for `lbl_80400C08`, including the format string and the original build date/time strings, plus the asm-side `__DSP_init_flag` sbss allocation.
Converted `__init_cpp` in `src/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.c` from a NON_MATCHING guarded C/asm pair to C guarded by `#pragma push`, `#pragma peephole off`, and `#pragma pop`.
Removed the asm fallback for `func_801C4E70` in `src/melee/gr/ground.c`; the existing C body now stands as matching. The removed note cited an epilogue ordering bug in `@frank`.

Factored HSD shadow texture-object setup into a new C helper `makeShadowTObj`, which calls `allocShadowTObj`, initializes `src`, `wrap_s`, `wrap_t`, `flags`, and `imagedesc`, and returns the configured `HSD_TObj*`.

Converted `HSD_ShadowAlloc` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.c` from a NON_MATCHING C/asm pair to C, using `makeShadowTObj()` and a local `f32 hack` to route viewport constants through existing labels.

Removed unused shadow asm constants `lbl_804DE768` and `lbl_804DE76C` for `0.5f` and `-0.5f` from `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.s`; the remaining viewport constants `lbl_804DE770` and `lbl_804DE774` are still referenced from C.
smart_moves: Moved data ownership from asm to C where possible: defining `__DSP_init_flag` in C eliminated an extern-related mismatch and allowed the matching `DSPInit` C body to replace inline asm.

Used explicit historical build date/time literals (`"Sep  8 2001"`, `"01:51:48"`) rather than compiler `__DATE__`/`__TIME__`, preserving the target binary strings and avoiding nondeterministic build output.
Applied `#pragma peephole off` narrowly around `__init_cpp`, preserving a clean C implementation while controlling compiler optimization/codegen enough to match the target.
Factored `makeShadowTObj` out of `HSD_ShadowAlloc`, likely aligning call/register/lifetime behavior with the target and reducing the amount of code that needed to match in one large function.

Used a temporary `f32 hack` and existing float labels for `HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4` arguments, with an explicit TODO documenting that this is a matching workaround until the whole file can use normal float constants.
Removed redundant asm-side constants once C literals or C-owned definitions generated the required data, shrinking asm debt instead of merely hiding NON_MATCHING blocks.
decomp_lessons: If a function is non-matching because globals are declared as `extern`, check whether the symbol should be defined in the C translation unit instead of allocated in an asm file.
For SDK/library functions that embed build timestamps, do not use live `__DATE__` and `__TIME__` macros when matching a fixed retail binary; use the exact target strings if evidenced by asm/data.
Small compiler pragmas can be an acceptable matching tactic when they are tightly scoped with push/pop around one sensitive function.
When a large allocation/init function does not match, splitting a coherent initialization subroutine into a helper can improve codegen and better reflect the original function boundaries.

Float constants can influence `.sdata2` layout and load behavior. Reusing existing labels or temporary locals may be necessary until a file is sufficiently converted for normal constants to land in the right places.

After replacing an asm fallback with matching C, also remove associated asm-only data and sbss symbols if C now owns or generates them; otherwise stale symbols can preserve unnecessary assembly dependencies.
naming_conventions: `__DSP_init_flag` remains the Dolphin SDK-style double-underscore global name and was promoted from extern declaration to C definition.
New helper `makeShadowTObj` follows existing camelCase baselib naming near `allocShadowTObj` rather than introducing an HSD-prefixed public API name.
Existing unresolved labels such as `lbl_804DE770` and `lbl_804DE774` remained label-style names where the PR did not provide semantic names.
The temporary variable was named `hack` and annotated with a TODO, signaling that it is a deliberate matching workaround rather than meaningful domain terminology.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted inline asm implementations for `DSPInit`, `__init_cpp`, `func_801C4E70`, and `HSD_ShadowAlloc` after making their C implementations match.
Replaced asm data label `lbl_80400C08` usage with direct string literals in the `__DSP_debug_printf` call.
Used `#pragma peephole off` around `__init_cpp` to influence instruction generation for the constructor-loop function.
Removed asm-side sbss allocation for `__DSP_init_flag` after defining it in C.
Kept viewport float constants loaded via `lbl_804DE770`/`lbl_804DE774` and a local `f32 hack` to match call argument codegen for `HSD_CObjSetViewportfx4`.
Removed shadow `.sdata2` labels for `0.5f` and `-0.5f` after normal C assignments `shadow->scaleS = 0.5F;` and `shadow->scaleT = -0.5F;` became acceptable.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
One review is recorded in counts, but no review body or actionable reviewer feedback is included in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find other Dolphin SDK functions where NON_MATCHING is caused by asm-owned globals that should be C definitions.
Search for uses of `__DATE__` or `__TIME__` that should be replaced with fixed target build strings for matching.
Search for narrowly applicable `#pragma peephole off` opportunities around constructor/destructor table loops.
Audit remaining `shadow.c` float-label workarounds and remove `hack` once the whole file is converted enough to use normal float constants.
Search for remaining inline asm fallbacks with comments blaming compiler epilogue ordering or externs; they may be fixable with modern matching tactics.
